articles.title: Heavy rain floods Jaipur and Kolkata: Indore closes 15 tourist sites for public safety; heatwave persists i... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Heavy rain floods roads, homes in Jaipur and Kolkata:Indore closes 15 tourist sites for public safety; heatwave persists in UP, Bihar
New Delhi/Bhopal/Jaipur/Lucknow9 hours ago
As Rajasthan awaits the full arrival of the monsoon, Jaipur was lashed by heavy rain on Thursday. Around 50mm of rain fell in nearly half an hour, flooding roads, stranding vehicles and inundating several homes and shops.
Kolkata also witnessed severe waterlogging after nearly an hour of rainfall. Up to a foot of water accumulated on major roads, while floodwater entered SSKM Hospital, causing difficulties for patients and attendants.
In Madhya Pradesh, access to 15 tourist sites in Indore has been suspended until 22 August due to the risk of rain-related accidents. In Shajapur, an elderly man was killed by lightning. The Meteorological Department said the monsoon is expected to cover the entire state within the next 2–4 days.
Meanwhile, the monsoon remains eight days behind schedule in Uttar Pradesh. It has been stalled near the Bihar border for the past 15 days, while heatwave conditions persisted in eight districts of UP. Bihar also continued to experience intense heat, with hot winds and strong sunshine affecting 13 districts.
See the current extent of the monsoon on the map

Weather forecast for the next 2 days
27 June
- Heavy rainfall is expected in parts of Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal and Sikkim.
- An orange alert has been issued for heavy rain in parts of Sikkim, Goa, Telangana and Karnataka.
- Warnings for heavy rainfall are also in place for Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand and Odisha.
- Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh and the Nagpur–Amravati region of Maharashtra remain under a heatwave alert for the fourth consecutive day.
28 June
- Heavy rain is likely across Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Odisha, Kerala, Goa, Tamil Nadu, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal and Sikkim.
- Bihar may witness thunderstorms with wind speeds of 50–60 km/h, while strong winds are also expected in Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, eastern Rajasthan and Tamil Nadu.
- Rajasthan is likely to receive rain accompanied by winds of 30–40 km/h, while heatwave conditions are expected to persist in parts of eastern Uttar Pradesh.
Weather condition of the states…
Monsoon to reach Bhopal, Ujjain in 2 days: Gwalior-Chambal, Sagar and Rewa to receive it last; rain and storm alert in 45 districts
A day after the southwest monsoon officially entered 15 districts of MP, its advance temporarily stalled on Wednesday. However, widespread rainfall continued across the state, with heavy showers and thunderstorms reported from several districts, including Shajapur. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued rain and thunderstorm alerts for 45 districts on Friday. Read More
Storm, rain alert for 27 Rajasthan districts: Jaipur roads waterlogged after 30-minute downpour, weather changes in Kota and Jodhpur
The pre-monsoon spell continued across Rajasthan on Thursday (June 25), bringing dust storms and rain to several parts of the state. Areas in the Jaipur, Ajmer, Kota, Bharatpur and Bikaner divisions witnessed strong winds in the afternoon, followed by rainfall. Read More

articles.title: Monsoon update: Heavy rain for Northeast, Kerala logs 33% rainfall deficit - News9live
articles.full_text:
Monsoon update: Heavy rain for Northeast, Kerala logs 33% rainfall deficit
The southwest monsoon is strengthening across India, with heavy rain forecast over the Northeast, western coast and central India. While several states are expected to receive widespread showers, Kerala has recorded a 33 per cent rainfall deficit and eastern Uttar Pradesh remains under a heatwave.
Monsoon in India | Image: PTI
New Delhi: The southwest monsoon is becoming more active across India, bringing widespread rainfall to several regions while leaving others struggling with heat and rainfall deficits.
Monsoon in India
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected over the Northeast on Friday, with Assam and Meghalaya likely to receive the heaviest showers.
Heavy rain is also forecast for Arunachal Pradesh, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, coastal Andhra Pradesh, coastal Karnataka, Konkan, Goa and Madhya Maharashtra. Moist winds from the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal are strengthening the monsoon, increasing the risk of flash floods and landslides in hilly areas.
The IMD has warned of thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across several states, including Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan, Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra and West Bengal. Bihar could witness thundersqualls with wind speeds of up to 60 kmph.
Despite the advancing monsoon, eastern Uttar Pradesh remains under a heatwave warning. Banda recorded the country’s highest maximum temperature at 44.2 degrees Celsius on June 24, showing that several regions are still awaiting meaningful rainfall.
Tamil Nadu rains
Tamil Nadu is expected to receive heavy rain in more than 10 districts, including Nilgiris, Coimbatore, Tiruppur, Theni, Dindigul, Tirunelveli and Kanyakumari from June 28. Chennai is also likely to witness light to moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorms. During the last 24 hours, Thammampatty in Salem recorded the state’s highest rainfall at 4 cm, followed by 3 cm in Veppanthattai, Perambalur.
Uttarakhand monsoon
In Uttarakhand, the monsoon is expected to enter through the Kumaon region over the next three to four days, nearly a week later than normal. Heavy rainfall is likely from June 28 onwards, particularly in the Kumaon region.
Kerala monsoon
Kerala continues to face a difficult season. The state has recorded a 33 per cent rainfall deficit so far, with Wayanad reporting the highest shortfall at 64 per cent. Only four districts — Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, Pathanamthitta and Thrissur — have received normal rainfall.
IMD officials attributed the weak monsoon to El Niño conditions and the absence of any Bay of Bengal depression during June, warning that the deficit could affect farming, groundwater levels and drinking water supply.
Monsoon in Rajasthan
Rajasthan has also witnessed below-normal rainfall, although light showers were reported in several districts. Jaipur received 16.4 mm of rain, while Phalodi remained the hottest place in the state at 41.4 degrees Celsius.
The weather department expects the southwest monsoon to advance further into Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh and Uttarakhand over the next few days, bringing more widespread rainfall across the country.
Saumya Agrawal has accumulated over four years of experience curating viral, trending, offbeat, and unconventional news stories. Her journalistic contributions span reputable media outlets, showcasing her ability to uncover viral content.

articles.title: Monsoon gathers pace; Delhi may see showers, Goa on heavy rain watch - CNBC TV18
articles.full_text:
The southwest monsoon is set to advance into more parts of Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh and Uttarakhand over the next few days, while Delhi is likely to witness another spell of light rain and thunderstorms on Friday, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
The IMD said conditions remain favourable for the monsoon to spread into more parts of the country during the next three to four days. Even as the monsoon continues its northward, the weather office has warned of heavy to very heavy rainfall along the west coast, across Northeast India and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim through the coming week.
In eastern India, Bihar remains under a heavy rainfall alert, while Odisha, Jharkhand and Gangetic West Bengal are likely to witness scattered rain and thunderstorms. The IMD has also warned that rainfall intensity will increase over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim later this weekend, with isolated extremely heavy rainfall likely between June 27 and 29.
Southern India is expected to remain under an active monsoon pattern. Widespread rainfall is forecast over Kerala, Lakshadweep and Coastal Karnataka, while Telangana, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and interior Karnataka are likely to experience thunderstorms accompanied by gusty winds.
For Delhi, the IMD has forecast a partly cloudy sky with a spell of very light to light rain accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and strong surface winds of 40-50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph, during the afternoon or evening. Maximum temperatures are expected to range between 39°C and 41°C, while minimum temperatures are likely to remain between 25°C and 27°C.
Heatwave conditions are likely to persist over East Uttar Pradesh, with severe heatwave conditions forecast in isolated pockets through June 27. Heatwave conditions are also expected over Jharkhand on Friday, even as rain continues to affect large parts of eastern and northeastern India.
